name: Motion & Interaction System
description: The Citron motion language. Motion behaves like light responding to touch: instant attack, long luminous decay. Every motion is purposeful; the signature curve is the brand's fingerprint.
principles: Motion communicates hierarchy and state changes — never decoration alone.
Fast by default. Users never wait for an animation to complete before acting.
One signature: fast attack, long decay (easing.signature). Reveals bloom like light; feedback snaps like a switch.
Choreograph, don't animate. Elements enter in reading order with staggered offsets, as one composed sequence.
Respect prefers-reduced-motion. All motion must be gracefully degradable.
Use CSS transitions for state changes. Reserve JS springs for drags, brand moments, and orchestrated sequences.

instant.usage: Immediate response. Color changes, opacity toggles with no motion.

fast.usage: Micro-interactions: button press, checkbox toggle, hover state changes.

normal.usage: Standard transitions: dropdown open, panel slide, tab switch.

slow.usage: Complex reveals: modal entry, accordion expand.

cinematic.usage: Brand-moment reveals: hero entrances, specimen choreography. Never for interaction feedback.

stagger.usage: Per-item offset for staggered entrances. Cap total sequence at 400ms.

default.usage: General purpose. Most transitions use this.

in.usage: Elements leaving the screen. Accelerates out of view.

out.usage: Elements entering the screen. Decelerates into position.

inOut.usage: Elements moving across screen. Smooth start and end.

signature.usage: THE CITRON CURVE. Near-instant attack, very long decay. Use for every reveal, hero entrance, and content bloom. This is the motion fingerprint of the brand.

swift.usage: Assertive position changes: shared-element moves, tab indicator slides, reordering.

springs.description: Spring presets for JS animation (Framer Motion / Motion One). CSS fallback: easing.signature.

responsive.usage: Interaction feedback — toggles, drags. Critically damped, zero overshoot.

gentle.usage: Panels, sheets, popovers — settled and calm.

bloom.usage: Brand moments — mascot, hero marks. The only spring allowed a hint of overshoot.

choreography.description: Rules for composing multi-element sequences. A view enters as one gesture, not a pile of animations.
rules: Entrance order follows reading order: eyebrow → headline → body → media → actions.
Stagger siblings by duration.stagger (40ms); cap the whole sequence at 400ms regardless of item count.
Parents fade + rise 12px with easing.signature over duration.slower; children inherit the reveal, they do not re-animate.
Exits are always faster than entrances (duration.fast, easing.in) and never staggered.
Only one cinematic-duration element per view — the hero. Everything else uses the interaction register.
Transform + opacity only. Never animate layout properties (width, height, top) except accordion height.

signatureReveal.css: .reveal { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(12px); animation: citron-reveal var(--inkblot-duration-slower) var(--ease-signature) forwards; } @keyframes citron-reveal { to { opacity: 1; transform: none; } }

transitions.description: Pre-defined transition patterns for common interactions

fadeIn.usage: Content appearing: toasts, tooltips, lazy-loaded images

fadeOut.usage: Content disappearing: dismissed toasts, closing tooltips

bloomUp.usage: THE signature entrance. Sections, cards, modals, any content reveal.

cardHover.effect: transform: translateY(-2px); box-shadow: var(--inkblot-shadow-md); on dark surfaces add var(--inkblot-shadow-glow-subtle)

citronGlow.trigger: hover / focus on primary actions over dark surfaces
citronGlow.effect: box-shadow: var(--inkblot-shadow-glow) fades in — the light-source moment

checkboxCheck.effect: Checkmark draws in via stroke-dashoffset animation

shimmer.effect: Gradient sweeps left to right across skeleton elements

focusRing.note: Use :focus-visible, not :focus, to avoid showing on mouse click

pageTransitions.rule: Use when navigating between views in SPAs. Keep under 400ms.

slideForward.in: translateX(24%) to translateX(0) + opacity 1 over 250ms easing.signature

slideBack.in: translateX(-24%) to translateX(0) + opacity 1 over 250ms easing.signature

reducedMotion.rule: Replace all animations with instant state changes. No transform animations. Opacity transitions allowed but must be <= 100ms.
reducedMotion.css: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, *::before, *::after { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; animation-iteration-count: 1 !important; transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; scroll-behavior: auto !important; } }
